preamble: The transition from a glottal stop to the vowel [a] is most famously represented by the adaptation of the Phoenician letter Aleph (a glottal stop) into the Greek Alpha (the vowel [a]).

examples.note: Repurposing of a glottal stop consonant as a vowel during script adoption.
The coloring of adjacent vowels by a laryngeal consonant often reconstructed as a glottal stop.

sources: The History of the Greek Alphabet
